user_outcome: Every spec-v1-era release record on the integration branch conforms to the v0.10.0 contract: quadrant 'chore' is migrated to 'quick' AND 'epic' to 'beast', in_scope/out_of_scope is present on every spec.json, each of the five spec-v1-era board.json files drops the now-derived tracks[].state and worktree fields (board-v1 pure plan), the one invalid 'feature' quadrant is fixed, index.md renders are refreshed, and the reader then enforces the v0.10.0 enum only — the data half of sworn#48. The sweep lands as a committed, re-runnable script so consumer repos (e.g. ~/projects/fired) can migrate their own incomplete releases with the same tool. Pre-spec-v1 legacy releases are explicitly untouched (Coach decision 2026-07-10: completed releases are not repaired).
rationale: sworn#48 items 2-3 data half. The issue counted ~28 live records using quadrant 'chore' across 2026-06-30-sworn-operational-readiness, 2026-07-01-release-hygiene, and 2026-07-01-render-drift-reconciliation; this release adds its own (S07 is 'chore' at planning; others are grind/puzzle/epic and unchanged). Backfill policy per the issue: historical specs get empty in_scope/out_of_scope arrays (schema-valid, lazy); this release's 12 specs already carry real content (backfilled at planning, 2026-07-02). The #48 aside is also in scope: one record carries invalid 'quadrant': 'feature' (reported near render-drift's S03-tui-chrome-rework — locate by grep, fix to the correct enum value, note the choice in the journal). FILE-CEILING NOTE: the sweep touches ~40+ record files, over the nominal 15-25 ceiling — accepted as a documented exception because every edit is a one-line mechanical data change from one sweep script; splitting by release would be artificial. DRIFT RISK: in-flight releases (operational-readiness tracks, release-hygiene T1) hold diverged copies of their records on track branches; this migration lands on release/v0.1.0 and reaches those tracks via /implement-slice Step-0 forward-merge self-heal — named here so the drift-gate behaviour is expected, not mysterious (the 2026-06-28 replan-propagation lesson). REPLAN AMENDMENT 2026-07-10: (1) the true chore/epic count is 42 record files across FIVE spec-v1-era releases — the original ~28 estimate missed 2026-07-01-loop-cli-ux (6 files) and undercounted; touchpoints updated. (2) Coach decision: pre-spec-v1 legacy releases (2026-06-19-safe-parallelism, 2026-06-27-conformance-foundation, and earlier markdown-era records — 106 slices with no spec.json) are NOT repaired; completed releases stay as-is, so their pre-existing doctor render-drift and timestamp findings remain, accepted. AC-05/AC-06 are scoped to the five spec-v1-era releases accordingly. (3) The sweep must be a committed re-runnable script, not a one-shot: ~/projects/fired carries incomplete releases needing the identical migration, run in that repo as follow-up work outside this release. V0.10.0 NOTE (2026-07-11 replan): S12's migration validates records against the S11-updated vendored schema (now v0.10.0, not v0.9.0 — T7 order S11 -> S15 -> S12 serialises this). The sweep already covers the five spec-v1-era releases, which now includes the new sibling S15-baton-version-handshake records (authored in current-vocab chore, migrated to quick with the rest). S12 remains the LAST T7 slice — it removes S11's normalise shim and tightens Validate to quick-only, so no sibling authors a retired-vocab record after it. PIN DECISIONS 2026-07-11 (S12 design review): (1) ears_pattern reshape FOLDED IN (AC-07) — v0.10.0 spec-v1's AC item allows only id/text/ears_pattern/test_refs (not sworn's old type/ears_keyword); the migration MAPS type->ears_pattern (preserving classification) and repoints ears.go, so EARS lint does not silently regress to all-Ubiquitous across the five releases (sworn#95). Homed here (not re-opening verified S11) because the ears.go code change must land atomically with the record reshape. (2) AC-02 converted to a zero-match feature-quadrant GUARD — zero such records exist (the original claim was stale).
in_scope: Quadrant data migration: every '"quadrant": "chore"' becomes 'quick' AND every '"quadrant": "epic"' becomes 'beast' under docs/release/ (spec.json + status.json, all releases including this one)
in_scope/out_of_scope presence backfill: empty arrays for historical specs (driver-contract's 12 already carry real content)
Fixing the single invalid 'quadrant': 'feature' record (the sworn#48 aside)

Removing S11's normalise-before-validate shim (the read-path chore->quick / epic->beast mapping + schema_version strip + board reshape) now that the on-disk data is migrated, leaving Validate strictly quick-only

Re-rendering index.md for each of the five spec-v1-era releases (2026-06-28-driver-contract, 2026-06-30-sworn-operational-readiness, 2026-07-01-loop-cli-ux, 2026-07-01-release-hygiene, 2026-07-01-render-drift-reconciliation) so the E-by-C column shows the migrated values

board.json pure-plan migration: each of the five spec-v1-era releases' board.json drops tracks[].state, tracks[].worktree_path/worktree_branch, and release worktree fields — the fields baton board-v1 (v0.9.0+, vendored at v0.10.0) removed as derived; sequenced AFTER S11 lands the deriving code so no binary reads a field that's gone

The migration sweep is a committed, re-runnable, idempotent script (scripts/migrate-records-v0.9.sh or equivalent) taking a docs/release root as its argument, so consumer repos (~/projects/fired) can run the identical migration on their incomplete releases

schema_version field strip: v0.10.0's spec-v1 and board-v1 set additionalProperties:false and no longer define schema_version ($schema carries the version) — remove it from every spec.json and board.json in the five spec-v1-era releases; status.json may keep it (slice-status-v1 has additionalProperties:true)

EARS field reshape (AC-07, Pin 1): map each acceptance criterion's retired 'type' -> canonical 'ears_pattern', drop 'ears_keyword', and repoint ears.go/classifySpecJSON to read 'ears_pattern' — so migrating to strict v0.10.0 spec-v1 PRESERVES EARS lint classification instead of degrading it to all-Ubiquitous (sworn#95). Coupled code+data change, homed here because S11 is verified and the code must land atomically with the record reshape
out_of_scope: Any schema or Go behaviour change beyond (a) removing S11's normalise shim + tightening Validate to quick-only, and (b) the ears.go/classifySpecJSON read-field change from 'ears_keyword' to 'ears_pattern' coupled to the AC-07 record reshape (Coach 2026-07-11, pin 1) — S11 owns the rest of the code half
Migrating records on in-flight TRACK branches directly — they receive the migration via forward-merge self-heal, never by cross-worktree edits (Rule 11)
board-v1/proof-v1 canonical-shape migration (baton#54, excluded by sworn#48)

Pre-spec-v1 legacy releases (2026-06-19-safe-parallelism, 2026-06-27-conformance-foundation, and earlier markdown-era records): completed releases are not repaired — Coach decision 2026-07-10; their existing doctor render-drift and timestamp findings stay as accepted, known findings
Running the migration script in consumer repos (~/projects/fired) — follow-up work executed in that repo after this release ships

Backfilling contracts.json onto historical releases — contracts.json is advisory + planner-emitted GOING FORWARD only (2026-07-11 replan, Baton v0.10.0). S12 does NOT emit or migrate contracts.json for any release; it does not scope-creep into 'emit contracts.json for every old release'
Vendoring or migrating the new contracts-v1 / assembly-proof-v1 schema FILES — S11 owns vendoring those (advisory); they are new schemas, not legacy records to migrate

effort_complexity.rationale: Mechanical sweep + renders + one tolerance removal; breadth not depth. Runs last in the release so no sibling track holds diverged copies of THIS release's records.

touchpoints: internal/lint/ (ears.go / classifySpecJSON — the ears_pattern read-field change)

risks.description: In-flight releases' track branches hold diverged record copies; migrating on the integration branch makes those tracks read 'behind'.
risks.mitigation: Expected and self-healing: /implement-slice Step 0 forward-merges release-wt/base into the track. The journal names every in-flight track touched so the next session isn't surprised (replan-propagation lesson, 2026-06-28).

risks.description: A missed record leaves the strict quick-only reader failing on live data after the tolerance is removed.
risks.mitigation: AC-01/AC-03 are grep-proven zero-match assertions run in the same slice that removes the tolerance; the repo-wide doctor/drift check is the backstop.

acceptance_criteria.text: After the migration, `grep -rn '"quadrant": "chore"' docs/release/` SHALL return zero matches, with every migrated record now reading 'quick', and `grep -rn '"quadrant": "epic"' docs/release/` likewise returning zero (all migrated to 'beast'); grind/puzzle untouched (the grep outputs are the slice's reachability artefact, captured in proof.json).

`grep -rn '"quadrant": "feature"' docs/release/` across the five spec-v1-era releases SHALL return zero matches (a regression guard, same shape as AC-01). The original 'correct the single feature-quadrant record' claim was stale — zero such records exist in JSON form (verified 2026-07-11); AC-02 is a zero-match GUARD against a future invalid-quadrant record, not a correction (Coach 2026-07-11, S12 review pin 2).

Every spec.json under docs/release/ SHALL contain in_scope and out_of_scope fields (empty arrays permitted for historical records) and SHALL validate against the vendored v0.10.0 spec-v1 schema — proven by a validation sweep whose output is captured in proof.json.
state.EffortComplexity.Validate SHALL accept only quick/grind/puzzle/beast (S11's normalise shim removed (Validate strictly quick-only)), and `go test ./internal/state/...` SHALL pass.

When `sworn render` is re-run for each of the five spec-v1-era releases (2026-06-28-driver-contract, 2026-06-30-sworn-operational-readiness, 2026-07-01-loop-cli-ux, 2026-07-01-release-hygiene, 2026-07-01-render-drift-reconciliation) after the migration, the committed index.md files SHALL match the render output (the fail-closed drift guard passes for those releases), with the refreshed renders committed in this slice. Pre-spec-v1 legacy releases are excluded (Coach decision 2026-07-10).

After the board.json migration, none of the five spec-v1-era releases' board.json files SHALL carry tracks[].state, tracks[].worktree_path/worktree_branch, or release-level worktree fields, and each of those five SHALL validate against the vendored v0.10.0 board-v1 (pure plan: tracks[] = id/slices/depends_on); the validation sweep output is captured in proof.json. Pre-spec-v1 legacy releases' board.json files are excluded (Coach decision 2026-07-10).

When the migration reshapes each acceptance criterion for strict v0.10.0 spec-v1 conformance (which allows only id/text/ears_pattern/test_refs), it SHALL MAP the retired sworn-local 'type' field to the canonical 'ears_pattern' field (ubiquitous->ubiquitous, event-driven->event-driven, state-driven->state-driven, optional-feature->optional-feature, complex->complex, unwanted->unwanted-behaviour, note->note) rather than dropping the classification, and SHALL drop the now-redundant 'ears_keyword'; internal (ears.go / classifySpecJSON) SHALL read 'ears_pattern' so `sworn lint` EARS classification is PRESERVED (no all-Ubiquitous degradation). A test SHALL assert lint classifies a migrated event-driven/unwanted-behaviour record correctly (not as Ubiquitous). sworn#95.
